message:
feat(signal): implement Unix signal dispatcher replacing Phase 0 stubs

The signal subsystem was previously stubbed out with Phase 0 placeholders
that returned "not implemented" errors for all signal operations. This
commit introduces a working signal infrastructure backed by signal-hook:

- Add signal-hook 0.3 dependency for safe cross-platform signal registration
- Implement SignalDispatcher: a global OnceLock-based dispatcher thread that
  receives process signals via signal-hook's iterator API and fans them out
  to per-kind async waiters using Notify + monotone delivery counters
- Signal::recv() now properly blocks until a signal is delivered, using an
  acquire/release delivery counter to ensure cancel-safety (no lost signals)
- ctrl_c() delegates to signal(SignalKind::Interrupt) instead of returning
  a stub error, making Ctrl+C handling functional on Unix
- is_available() now performs a real probe on Unix (attempts dispatcher init)
- Update all tests from asserting errors to asserting success on Unix,
  with cfg(not(unix)) fallbacks preserving unsupported-error assertions
- Add signal_recv_observes_delivery test using dispatcher inject() for
  deterministic testing without sending real signals
- Update module docs from "Phase 0" language to "Platform Behavior"

Non-Unix builds continue to return unsupported errors with the same API
surface, maintaining forward compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -144,6 +144,8 @@ rustls-pemfile = { version = "2.2", optional = true }
 base64 = "0.22.1"
 # SHA-1 hashing for WebSocket Sec-WebSocket-Accept calculation (RFC 6455)
 sha1 = "0.10"
+# Cross-platform signal registration (safe wrapper around OS signal handlers)
+signal-hook = "0.3"
 # X.509 certificate parsing for SPKI extraction
 x509-parser = { version = "0.18", optional = true }
 # Optional TOML config file support for RuntimeBuilder
